Use for polite requests. Example: 물 주세요: Use for destination or direction. Example: 홍대로 가주세요 = Please go to Hongdae.
Makes speech polite. Example: 죄송해요 = I am sorry.
Use for polite present speech. Example: 감사해요 = Thank you.
Use after a noun to answer or order politely. Example: 포장이요 = To go, please.
Use casually with friends or fans. Example: 찐이야 = It is real.
Use for I will or a soft promise. Example: 먹고 갈게요 = I will eat here.
Connects actions: and. Example: 먹고 갈게요 = I will eat and go.
Use for where something happens. Example: 카페에서 만나요 = Meet at the cafe.
Softens I know or do not know. Example: 모르겠어요 = I am not sure.
Makes a verb negative. Example: 안 돼요 = It is not okay.
A more formal way to say not. Example: 어렵지 않아요 = It is not hard.
Formal polite ending. Example: 감사합니다 = Thank you.
Means want to. Example: 가고 싶어요 = I want to go.
Casual request with friends or fans. Example: 알려줘 = Tell me.
